case_title: RAKESH SINGH VS. ETHOS LENDING, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL

docket_entries.description: MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT ETHOS LENDING, LLCS DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100004083) FILED BY DEFENDANT ETHOS LENDING LLC
NOTICE NOTICE OF ERRATA (TRANSACTION ID # 100004083) FILED BY DEFENDANT ETHOS LENDING LLC
OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 15341010) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SINGH, RAKESH

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ETHOS LENDING LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AND THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. IN REVIEWING A DEMURRER THE COURT ONLY EXAMINES THE LEGAL SUFFICIENCY OF A CAUSE OF ACTION, NOT THE TRUTH OF THE EVIDENCE. (QUELIMANE CO. V. STEWART TITLE GUARANTY CO. (1998) 19 CAL.4TH 26.) PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR A VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE 203. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANT KNOWINGLY AND WILLFULLY FAILED TO PAY WAGES OWED TO PLAINTIFF WHEN PLAINTIFF RESIGNED ON FEBRUARY 19, 2015, OR WITHIN 72 HOURS AFTER THE RESIGNATION. (FAC 29-35.) CONTRARY TO DEFENDANT'S POSITION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ADMITTED IN THE COMPLAINT THAT THERE IS A GOOD FAITH DISPUTE REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF WAGES OWED. ADDITIONALLY, THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT UNCERTAIN. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO PLEAD FACTS STATING WHICH STATUTORY OR CONSTITUTIONAL PROVISIONS WERE VIOLATED. (GREEN V. RALEE ENGINEERING CO. (1998) 19 CAL.4TH 66, 83 (IN WRONGFUL TERMINATION CASES WE HAVE REJECTED PUBLIC POLICY CLAIMS THAT WERE LARGELY UNACCOMPANIED BY CITATIONS TO SPECIFIC STATUTORY OR CONSTITUTIONAL PROVISIONS).) ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED FACTS THAT DEFENDANT RETALIATED AGAINST HIM FOR COMPLAINING ABOUT NOT BEING PAID PROPERLY, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT STATED WHICH OF HIS STATUTORY RIGHTS WERE VIOLATED OR WHAT STATUTES DEFENDANT VIOLATED. (FAC 26-31, 53-61.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. In re: DEFENDANT ETHOS LENDING LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled and the demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained, with ten days leave to amend. In reviewing a demurrer the court only examines the legal sufficiency of a cause of action, not the truth of the evidence. (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26.) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action for a violation of Labor Code 203. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant knowingly and willfully failed to pay wages owed to Plaintiff when Plaintiff resigned on February 19, 2015, or within 72 hours after the resignation. (FAC 29-35.) Contrary to Defendant's position, Plaintiff has not admitted in the complaint that there is a good faith dispute regarding the amount of wages owed. Additionally, the second cause of action is not uncertain. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to plead facts stating which statutory or constitutional provisions were violated. (Green v. Ralee Engineering Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 66, 83 (in wrongful termination cases we have rejected public policy claims that were largely unaccompanied by citations to specific statutory or constitutional provisions).) Although Plaintiff has alleged facts that Defendant retaliated against him for complaining about not being paid properly, Plaintiff has not stated which of his statutory rights were violated or what statutes Defendant violated. (FAC 26-31, 53-61.) Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
